zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hay於1510年1月1日出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾哈丁頓時，她的父親Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester24歲，母親Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus23歲。她於1527年4月10日與George Seton 6th Lord Seton在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾西頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1538年2月在蘇格蘭費伏洛斯享年28歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭費伏洛斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Hay于1510年1月1日出生在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾哈丁頓时，她的父亲Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester24岁，母亲Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus23岁。她于1527年4月10日与George Seton 6th Lord Seton在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾西頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1538年2月在苏格兰費伏洛斯享年28岁，埋葬在苏格兰費伏洛斯。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hay was born on 1 January 1510, in Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, her father, Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester, was 24 and her mother, Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus, was 23. She married George Seton 6th Lord Seton on 10 April 1527, in Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died in February 1538, in Culross, Fife, Scotland, at the age of 28, and was buried in Culross, Fife, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Hay est née le 1 janvier 1510, à Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé George Seton 6th Lord Seton le 10 avril 1527, à Seton, Haddingtonshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en février 1538, à Culross, Fife, Écosse, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterrée à Culross, Fife, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Hay am 1. Januar 1510 in Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester 24 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus 23. Sie hat am 10. April 1527 in Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland George Seton 6th Lord Seton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb im Februar 1538 im Alter von 28 Jahren in Culross, Fife, Scotland und wurde in Culross, Fife, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hay è nata il il 1 gennaio 1510 nel Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, sua padre, Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato George Seton 6th Lord Seton il 10 aprile 1527, nel Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel febbraio 1538, nel Culross, Fife, Scotland, all'età di 28 anni, e fu sepolta a Culross, Fife, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hayが1510年1月1日にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアハッドイントンで生まれた時，父Sir John Hay -III Lord Yesterは24歳，母Elizabeth Douglas of  Angusは23歳であった。1527年4月10日にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアセトンでGeorge Seton 6th Lord Setonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1538年2月にスコットランドフィフクウルロスで28歳で死去し，スコットランドフィフクウルロスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hay(이)가 1510년 1월 1일 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 하딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1527년 4월 10일 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 세턴에서 George Seton 6th Lord Seton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1538년 2월 스컷랜드 피프 컬로스에서 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 피프 컬로스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hay nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1510, em Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com George Seton 6th Lord Seton em 10 de abril de 1527, em Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em fevereiro de 1538, em Culross, Fife, Scotland, com 28 anos, e foi sepultada em Culross, Fife, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hay родилась 1 января 1510 г. в местности хаддингтон, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Seton 6th Lord Seton 10 апреля 1527 г. в местности сетон, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла февраль 1538 г. в местности кулросс, фиф, Шотландия в возрасте 28 лет и была похоронена в местности кулросс, фиф, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Hay nació el 1 de enero de 1510, en Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus, tenía 23 años. Se casó con George Seton 6th Lord Seton el 10 de abril de 1527, en Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en febrero de 1538, en Culross, Fife, Scotland, a la edad de 28 años, y fue sepultada en Culross, Fife, Scotland.
